title: ego lite: I gave Claude Code & Codex a browser to run web automation — here's what happened
summary: ego lite is a Chromium-based browser that provides AI agents with isolated workspaces for web automation while preserving the user's normal browsing context. It integrates with tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor, offers semantic webpage snapshots, and supports JavaScript-based workflow composition.
writeup: The video tests ego lite, a Chromium-based browser that gives AI coding agents isolated workspaces for web automation that run separately from a user's own browsing. It shows how agents like Claude Code and Codex compose browser workflows in JavaScript, with semantic webpage snapshots provided to help the agents navigate complex UI and handle iframes. The point of interest for those tracking agentic tooling is the separation between agent automation and normal browsing, plus the snapshot approach as an alternative to raw DOM parsing for grounding actions on a page.
